description_long: This object is an antique flintlock pistol, characterized by its long, octagonal barrel and ornately carved wooden grip. The frame and barrel are constructed from dark, aged metal, showing signs of patina and wear consistent with historical firearms. The grip is made of wood, featuring a curved ergonomic shape and visible grain patterns, likely for improved handling. A prominent hammer and trigger mechanism are visible, with the hammer positioned above the grip and the trigger guard extending below. The barrel is relatively slender and extends well beyond the grip, typical of early single-shot pistols. There are no visible modern attachments or magazine systems, and the overall design is consistent with firearms from the 18th or early 19th century.
description: An antique flintlock pistol with a long, dark metal barrel and a curved wooden grip, featuring a classic hammer and trigger mechanism.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, the curved wooden grip, and the hammer mechanism.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow profile of the barrel and the rounded grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the trigger guard, hammer, and the smooth curvature of the grip.
description_view_3: Front view down the barrel, emphasizing the circular muzzle and the alignment of the barrel with the grip.
